exercises.name: Ab Rollout
exercises.description: Kneel and roll forward with hands on a bench, extending abs fully.
formCues: Go only as far as you can maintain form
Pull back with abs, don't push
commonMistakes: Avoid going too far too soon

exercises.name: Abductor Walk
exercises.description: With a band around legs, step sideways, keeping tension to target abductors.
formCues: Step sideways keeping tension on band
Walk a set distance then return

exercises.name: Adductor Plank
exercises.description: In a side plank, lift top leg slightly, engaging adductors for stability.
formCues: Side plank position with top leg crossed under
commonMistakes: Don't put too much weight on bottom shoulder

exercises.name: Adductor Side Raise
exercises.description: Lie on side, lift bottom leg up against gravity, isolating adductors.
formCues: Stand with band around ankles or ankles crossed
commonMistakes: Don't use momentum to swing leg

exercises.name: Archer Pull-Up
exercises.description: One arm pulls while the other extends sideways, building unilateral back strength.
formCues: Shift weight to one side on pull
commonMistakes: Avoid helping with the non-working arm
Don't shorten the range of motion

exercises.name: Archer Push-Up
exercises.description: One arm extends to the side while the other bends, mimicking an archer's draw, for unilateral chest strength.
formCues: Shift weight to one arm as you lower
Keep other arm straight like an archer's bow
Push back to center from each side
commonMistakes: Avoid letting the working elbow flare
Don't touch ground with non-working hand

exercises.name: Assisted Pistol Squat
exercises.description: Use a band for support while performing a single-leg squat.
formCues: Stand on one leg, other extended forward
Lower as low as possible on single leg

exercises.name: Assisted Pull-Up
exercises.description: Use resistance bands to assist in pulling up, ideal for beginners.
formCues: Use band or machine for support
Focus on proper form not just reps
commonMistakes: Don't rely too much on assistance

exercises.name: Australian Pull Up
exercises.description: A bodyweight row variation performed on a bar set at waist height, requiring you to lie beneath the bar and pull your chest up while keeping your body in a straight line - excellent for building back strength.
formCues: Position body under bar, feet on ground
Pull chest to bar while keeping body straight

exercises.name: Back Extension
exercises.description: Lie face down on a bench, lift torso up to work lower back muscles.
formCues: Lie face down on hyperextension bench
commonMistakes: Don't round your back during descent

exercises.name: Back Lever
exercises.description: Hang upside down, body parallel to ground, engaging back and posterior chain.
formCues: Start with tucked position on ring or bar

exercises.name: Bear Crawl
exercises.description: Crawl on hands and feet with knees off ground, a full-body strength and mobility move.
formCues: Start on hands and feet, knees hovering
Move forward with opposite hand and foot
Keep hips level and low to ground
commonMistakes: Don't let hips sag or pike
Avoid lifting one knee too high

exercises.name: Bench Calf Raise
exercises.description: Stand on a bench edge, raise heels for greater range, targeting calves.
formCues: Stand on edge of bench with heels hanging
Rise up onto balls of feet as high as possible
Lower heels below level of bench

exercises.name: Bench Dip
exercises.description: Hands on a bench, feet on ground, dip down to work triceps with less intensity.
formCues: Sit on edge of bench, hands gripping edge
Slide butt off bench, arms extended
Push back up to starting position
commonMistakes: Don't place feet too far forward

exercises.name: Biceps Curl
exercises.description: Using resistance bands, curl hands toward shoulders to isolate biceps.
formCues: Stand tall, hold weight with underhand grip

exercises.name: Bicycles
exercises.description: A dynamic core exercise performed lying on your back with legs in cycling motion while bringing opposite elbow to knee, providing comprehensive abdominal engagement through rotational movement patterns.
formCues: Lie on back, hands behind head

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.description: From all fours, extend opposite arm and leg, balancing to engage deep core muscles.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.description: One foot elevated behind on a bench, squat with front leg to target quads.
formCues: One foot behind on bench or step
Lower hips until front knee at 90 degrees
Drive through front heel to return
commonMistakes: Don't let front knee go past toes too far

exercises.name: Burpee
exercises.description: Squat, jump back to plank, push-up, then jump up—a full-body conditioning move.
formCues: Jump feet forward and explode up
commonMistakes: Don't sag on the plank position

exercises.name: Calf Raise
exercises.description: Stand, lift heels off ground, working calves with bodyweight in a simple motion.
formCues: Rise up onto toes as high as possible

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.description: Palms facing you, pull up to engage biceps more than lats, a classic back exercise.
commonMistakes: Avoid going too shallow at top

exercises.name: Clamshell
exercises.description: Lie on side with knees bent, open knees like a clam to work abductors.
formCues: Lie on side, knees bent at 90 degrees
Keep feet together, lift top knee

exercises.name: Clap Push-Up
exercises.description: An explosive push-up where you push off the ground hard enough to clap hands mid-air, building chest power and speed.
formCues: Clap hands at peak of movement
commonMistakes: Don't hesitate to clap - be decisive

exercises.name: Close-Grip Push-Up
exercises.description: Hands close together in a push-up, shifting focus to triceps over chest.

exercises.name: Commando Pull-Up
exercises.description: Alternate grip pull-up, pulling head to one side then the other, targeting back and biceps.
formCues: Grip rings or bars one hand each side
commonMistakes: Don't let one side do all the work

exercises.name: Cossack Squat
exercises.description: Wide stance squat shifting weight side to side, stretching and strengthening adductors.
formCues: Stand wide, shift weight to one side
Squat deeply on one leg while other straight
Keep heel down on squatting leg
commonMistakes: Don't lift heel on squatting leg

exercises.name: Crow Pose
exercises.description: Similar to frog stand but with knees outside elbows, improving wrist and core strength.
formCues: Place knees on backs of upper arms
commonMistakes: Don't put full weight on wrists

exercises.name: Crunch
exercises.description: Lie on back, curl shoulders toward pelvis to contract abs directly.
formCues: Hands behind head or crossed on chest
commonMistakes: Avoid going too high too soon

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.description: Lie on back, extend opposite arm and leg while keeping core braced, targeting deep abs.
formCues: Lie on back, arms extended up
Lower opposite arm and leg slowly
Keep lower back pressed to floor
commonMistakes: Don't let back arch off floor
Don't push too hard with limbs

exercises.name: Dead Hang
exercises.description: Hang from a bar as long as possible, building forearm endurance and grip.
formCues: Grip bar with both hands, arms extended

exercises.name: Decline Push-Up
exercises.description: Feet elevated, hands on ground, shifting focus to upper chest and shoulders in a push-up.
formCues: Focus on upper chest and shoulders
commonMistakes: Don't let hips sag below shoulder line
Avoid going too deep too soon

exercises.name: Diamond Push-Up
exercises.description: Hands close together forming a diamond shape, targeting the inner chest and triceps with a challenging push-up variation.
formCues: Form diamond with thumbs and index fingers
Keep elbows close to your ribs
commonMistakes: Don't go too shallow in the movement

exercises.name: Donkey Kick
exercises.description: On all fours, kick one leg back and up, isolating the glutes for strength and tone.
formCues: Lift one leg with knee bent at 90 degrees
commonMistakes: Don't extend the leg too much

exercises.name: Dragon Flag
exercises.description: Lie on a bench, lift legs and hips straight up, then lower slowly, an advanced ab move.
formCues: Lie on bench, grip behind head
Lift entire body as one straight line

exercises.name: Finger Pull-Up
exercises.description: Pull up using only fingertips on a bar, enhancing forearm and grip strength.
formCues: Build up gradually from regular pull-ups

exercises.name: Fire Hydrant
exercises.description: On all fours, lift one leg out to the side, mimicking a dog at a hydrant, for abductors.
formCues: Lift one leg out to side, knee bent at 90

exercises.name: Flutter
exercises.description: An ab isolation exercise performed lying on your back where you alternate raising and lowering legs in a fluttering motion, targeting lower abdominals and hip flexors for core strength development.
formCues: Lie on back, arms by sides
Alternate legs in small scissoring motion
commonMistakes: Don't let legs sag to floor

exercises.name: Frog Stand
exercises.description: Balance on hands with knees resting on elbows, a beginner step toward planche.
formCues: Squat low with hands on floor between feet
Place knees on arms for support
Lift one foot, then other to hover
commonMistakes: Avoid putting too much weight on shoulders

exercises.name: Front Lever
exercises.description: Hold body parallel to the ground from a bar, a static exercise for back and core strength.
formCues: Hang from bar with straight arms
Raise body until parallel to ground
commonMistakes: Don't let legs drop below horizontal

exercises.name: Front Lever Pull
exercises.description: From a hang, pull into a front lever and hold briefly, building dynamic strength.
formCues: Pull body up toward bar with straight arms

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Lie on back, lift hips by squeezing glutes, a basic activation exercise for glute strength.
formCues: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat
Lift hips until shoulders to knees in line

exercises.name: Glute-Ham Raise
exercises.description: On a bench, curl body up from knees, an advanced hamstring and glute exercise.
formCues: Anchor feet under pads, knees on pad
Lower body by extending at knee
Use hamstrings to pull back up
commonMistakes: Don't let hips sag too low

exercises.name: Hammer Pull Up
exercises.description: An advanced pull-up variation using gymnastic rings or a fat grip that emphasizes bicep engagement through the supinated (hammer) grip position at the top of each repetition.
commonMistakes: Don't let palms rotate during pull

exercises.name: Hamstring Curl with Band
exercises.description: Lie prone, curl legs against band resistance, isolating hamstrings effectively.
formCues: Attach band to ankle, anchor opposite end
Lie prone or stand, curl heel toward glute
commonMistakes: Don't use momentum to curl up

exercises.name: Handstand
exercises.description: Balance on hands with feet in the air, building shoulder stability and strength.
formCues: Start with wall handstand for stability
commonMistakes: Avoid walking feet too far down wall

exercises.name: Handstand Push-Up
exercises.description: From a handstand, lower head to ground and push back up, a powerful shoulder exercise.
formCues: Kick up to handstand against wall
Push back up with triceps and shoulders
commonMistakes: Don't walk feet down wall too far

exercises.name: Hanging Knee Raise
exercises.description: Hang from a bar, raise knees to chest, targeting hip flexors and abs.
formCues: Hang from bar with straight arms

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.description: Hang from a bar, raise straight legs to 90 degrees, focusing on lower abs.
formCues: Raise legs to horizontal or higher
commonMistakes: Don't let legs drop too fast

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.description: Rest shoulders on a bench, thrust hips up with feet flat, focusing on glute power.
formCues: Upper back on bench, feet on ground
Drive through heels to lift hips

exercises.name: Hollow Body Hold
exercises.description: Lie on back, lift arms and legs slightly, forming a 'banana' shape to work abs.
formCues: Lie on back, arms extended overhead
Lift shoulders and legs off ground
commonMistakes: Don't let back arch off floor

exercises.name: Hollow Rock
exercises.description: Rock back and forth in a hollow body position, enhancing core stability.
formCues: Keep lower back pressed to ground
commonMistakes: Don't let back come off floor

exercises.name: Human Flag
exercises.description: Hold body sideways from a pole, an extreme oblique and shoulder exercise.
formCues: Grip pole or bar with one hand high, one low
Keep straight line from head to feet

exercises.name: In and Out
exercises.description: A dynamic core exercise performed from a plank position where you jump your feet forward toward hands then back out, building explosive core strength and coordination for advanced calisthenics movements.
formCues: Jump feet out to wide position

exercises.name: Incline Push-Up
exercises.description: Hands on an elevated surface reduce difficulty, focusing on lower chest with a standard push-up motion.
formCues: Body forms straight line from head to heels
Use a height that allows proper form

exercises.name: Jump Calf Raise
exercises.description: Explosively jump while raising heels, building calf power and explosiveness.
formCues: Land softly on balls of feet

exercises.name: Jump Squat
exercises.description: Squat down and explode upward into a jump, building explosive quad power.
formCues: Land softly on balls of feet
commonMistakes: Don't let knees collapse inward on landing

exercises.name: Jumping Jack
exercises.description: Jump with arms and legs spreading out and in, a classic full-body warm-up.
formCues: Jump feet out while raising arms

exercises.name: Korean Dip
exercises.description: Dip on a straight bar with body behind, an advanced triceps and chest exercise.

exercises.name: L-Sit
exercises.description: Hold legs straight out while supporting on hands, targeting abs and hip flexors.
formCues: Sit between parallel bars or rings
Press down through hands, lift legs

exercises.name: Leg Raise
exercises.description: Lie on back, lift straight legs to 90 degrees, engaging hip flexors and lower abs.
formCues: Lie on floor, hands under hips
commonMistakes: Don't arch your back off floor

exercises.name: Mountain Climber
exercises.description: In plank, rapidly bring knees to chest, working core, shoulders, and legs.
formCues: Keep hips level and core tight
commonMistakes: Don't let hips pike or sag
Avoid lifting one hip too high
Don't rush at expense of form

exercises.name: Muscle-Up
exercises.description: Pull up and transition to a dip on a bar or rings, a full-body gymnastic move.
formCues: Start with kip or explosive pull-up
Transition quickly at top of pull

exercises.name: Muscle-Up Negative
exercises.description: Lower slowly from the top of a muscle-up, a progression for the full move.
formCues: Start at top of muscle-up (dip position)

exercises.name: Natural Glute-Ham Raise
exercises.description: Kneel and lower body using hamstrings without equipment, a bodyweight variation.
formCues: Lie prone, anchor feet under furniture
Pull body up using hamstrings and glutes
commonMistakes: Don't use momentum to pull up

exercises.name: Negative Pull-Up
exercises.description: Slowly lower from the top of a pull-up, building strength for full pull-ups.
formCues: Start at top position with chin over bar

exercises.name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
exercises.description: Kneel, lower torso forward with feet anchored, targeting hamstrings with eccentric strength.
formCues: Kneel, anchor feet under stable object
Lower body with control using hamstrings
Use hands to push back up initially
commonMistakes: Don't let knees come off ground
Don't go too deep too soon

exercises.name: One-Arm Chin-Up
exercises.description: Pull up with one arm on a bar, an extreme biceps and back challenge.
formCues: Use resistance band for assistance initially

exercises.name: One-Arm Handstand
exercises.description: Balance on one hand, an advanced shoulder and core stability challenge.
formCues: Practice against wall one arm at a time
commonMistakes: Avoid using momentum to kick up

exercises.name: One-Arm Pull-Up
exercises.description: Pull your body up with one arm, an advanced move for back and biceps.
formCues: Use resistance band for help initially

exercises.name: One-Arm Push-Up
exercises.description: Performed with one arm, requiring significant chest, shoulder, and core strength.

exercises.name: Parallel Bar Support
exercises.description: Hold body upright between parallel bars, building shoulder and triceps stability.

exercises.name: Pike Push-Up
exercises.description: Bend at hips with feet on ground, pushing up to target shoulders in a vertical press.
formCues: Walk feet closer, lift hips high
Lower head toward floor by bending elbows
commonMistakes: Don't let hips sag during movement
Avoid bending too much at waist

exercises.name: Pistol Squat
exercises.description: Squat on one leg with the other extended forward, requiring quad strength and balance.
formCues: Stand on one leg, other extended forward
Keep chest up and core tight
Use arm for balance if needed

exercises.name: Planche
exercises.description: Hold body parallel to ground on hands, an intense shoulder and chest static exercise.
formCues: Full planche - body parallel to ground

exercises.name: Planche Lean
exercises.description: A foundational planche progression where you lean your body forward at an angle while supporting weight on your hands, building the shoulder and core strength necessary for the full planche position.
commonMistakes: Avoid going too far too soon

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.description: Hold a straight body position on forearms and toes, engaging abs for stability.
formCues: Forearms on ground, elbows under shoulders
Keep body in straight line from head to heels
commonMistakes: Avoid piking hips up too high

exercises.name: Plank Knee to Elbow
exercises.description: A plank variation where you bring alternating knees toward opposite elbows while maintaining a stable plank position, engaging deep core muscles including obliques and transverse abdominis for improved stability.
commonMistakes: Don't let hips pike or sag

exercises.name: Plank Side to Side
exercises.description: A lateral plank variation where you shift weight side to side while maintaining one elbow support, building oblique strength and improving unilateral core stability for advanced bodyweight movements.
formCues: Start in high plank with feet wide
Rotate body to touch one hip to floor
Return to center and rotate other side

exercises.name: Plank with Leg Lift
exercises.description: In a plank, lift one leg at a time, enhancing transverse abdominis stability.
formCues: Lift one leg without rotating hips

exercises.name: Plyo Push-Up
exercises.description: Push explosively to lift hands off the ground, enhancing chest and shoulder power.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
exercises.description: Hang from a bar and pull your chin above it, targeting lats, biceps, and upper back with a full range of motion.
formCues: Grab bar with overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulders
commonMistakes: Don't shrug shoulders instead of pulling

exercises.name: Push-Up
exercises.description: A foundational exercise where you lower your chest to the ground and push back up, engaging the chest, shoulders, and triceps.
formCues: Keep your core tight throughout the movement
Maintain a straight line from head to heels
Exhale as you push up, inhale as you lower
Keep elbows at 45 degrees to your body
commonMistakes: Don't let hips sag or pike up
Avoid flaring elbows out to 90 degrees
Don't stop halfway through the movement

exercises.name: Reach Up
exercises.description: A mobility and core activation exercise performed standing or seated where you reach arms overhead while arching the back slightly, promoting thoracic spine extension and shoulder mobility essential for planche progression.

exercises.name: Reverse Hyperextension
exercises.description: Lie face down, lift legs up from a bench, targeting lower back and glutes.
formCues: Lie prone, hold bar behind head

exercises.name: Ring Chin-Up
exercises.description: Chin-up on unstable rings, increasing biceps and stabilizer engagement.
formCues: Grip rings with palms facing you

exercises.name: Ring Dip

formCues: Lower with control on unstable rings
commonMistakes: Don't let rings rotate too much

exercises.name: Ring Row
exercises.description: Pull body up from a low hang on rings, targeting back and biceps with adjustable difficulty.
formCues: Hang from rings with arms extended
commonMistakes: Don't let rings rotate too much

exercises.name: Rows
exercises.description: A fundamental horizontal pulling movement performed while hanging from rings or parallel bars, engaging lats, rhomboids, and rear delts with controlled upward motion of the torso.
formCues: Bend over slightly with back flat
Pull weight to lower chest or abs

exercises.name: Russian Twist
exercises.description: Sit, twist torso side to side with feet off ground, engaging obliques.
formCues: Sit with knees bent, feet lifted
Lean back slightly to engage core
commonMistakes: Don't rotate lower back instead of abs

exercises.name: Shoulder Tap
exercises.description: In a plank, tap opposite shoulder with one hand, engaging shoulders and core.
formCues: Tap opposite shoulder with each hand
commonMistakes: Don't let hips rotate or pike
Avoid lifting one leg for reach

exercises.name: Side Hanging Raise
exercises.description: Hang from a bar, raise legs sideways to target obliques.
formCues: Hang from bar, lean to one side

exercises.name: Side Lunge
exercises.description: Step to the side and squat, targeting adductors and improving lateral mobility.
formCues: Step out to one side into lunge
Bend working knee, keep heel down
commonMistakes: Don't let toe of working foot turn in

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description: Support body on one forearm and side of foot, holding to target obliques.
formCues: Lie on side, prop up on one forearm
Stack feet or stagger for stability
Lift hips to form straight line

exercises.name: Side-Lying Leg Raise
exercises.description: Lie on side, lift top leg up, isolating abductors for hip stability.
formCues: Lift top leg keeping it straight
Lower slowly to just above ground

exercises.name: Single-Leg Calf Raise
exercises.description: Lift heel on one leg, increasing calf strength and stability unilaterally.
formCues: Stand on one leg, other slightly forward
Rise up onto ball of standing foot

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Lift hips with one leg extended, targeting glutes unilaterally with added challenge.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift

formCues: Stand on one leg, slight knee bend
Hinge at hip, extend other leg back

exercises.name: Skin the Cat

formCues: Hang from bar with rings or rig
Walk feet forward while rotating body
Lower until back is against bar

exercises.name: Skull Crushers

formCues: Lie on bench, hold barbell overhead
Lower bar toward forehead by bending elbows

exercises.name: Spiderman Push-Up
exercises.description: Bring one knee to elbow during a push-up, engaging chest and obliques simultaneously.

exercises.name: Squat
exercises.description: Bend knees and hips to lower body, then stand, a basic quad and glute exercise.

exercises.name: Standing Abduction
exercises.description: With a band around legs, push one leg out to the side against resistance.
formCues: Lift one leg out to side keeping straight

exercises.name: Step-Up

Drive through one foot to step up
Bring other foot to stand beside it

exercises.name: Straddle L-Sit
exercises.description: Hold legs spread wide in an L-sit, advancing core and hip flexor strength.
formCues: Sit between bars, legs spread wide
Keep core engaged and back flat
commonMistakes: Don't use arms to pull up

exercises.name: Straddle Planche
exercises.description: Legs spread wide in a planche hold, increasing shoulder demand.
formCues: Start in push-up position with wide stance
Lift legs with knees slightly bent outward

exercises.name: Sumo Squat
exercises.description: Wide stance squat with toes out, emphasizing adductors and inner thighs.
formCues: Lower hips down and between legs

exercises.name: Superman Hold

Lift chest and legs off ground

Focus on grip and shoulder stability

exercises.name: Towel Pull-Up

exercises.name: Tricep Dip
exercises.description: Lower body between parallel bars and push back up, targeting triceps effectively.

exercises.name: Triceps Extension

exercises.name: Tuck Jump

exercises.name: Wall Handstand

exercises.name: Wall Sit

Avoid letting back slide down wall

exercises.name: Weighted Calf Raise

exercises.name: Weighted Dip

exercises.description: Add weight across hips during a hip thrust, intensifying glute activation.

exercises.name: Wide Pull-Up
exercises.description: Hands wider than shoulders to maximize lat engagement during the pull-up motion.

exercises.name: Wide Push-Up
exercises.description: Hands placed wider than shoulders to emphasize chest activation over triceps, performed by lowering and raising the body.

Don't rotate hips side to side

exercises.name: Windshield Wipers
exercises.description: Hang from a bar, swing legs side to side like wipers, working obliques.
formCues: Lie on back, arms out to sides
Lift legs and bring to one side
Lower control, then bring other side
commonMistakes: Don't let shoulders lift off ground

routines.name: Push Training
routines.description: Entrenamiento enfocado en músculos de empuje: pecho, hombros y tríceps

routines.name: Pull Training
routines.description: Entrenamiento enfocado en músculos de jalón: espalda, bíceps y antebrazos

routines.name: Core Training
routines.description: Entrenamiento específico para fortalecer el core: abdominales, lumbares y estabilidad
